summary: Soundboard capture of a strong show (extended Bertha, epic The Other One & Comes A Time) hampered by intermittent Keith‑piano distortion.
review: The performance is widely praised – an extended Bertha, the cryptical sections of The Other One and a moving Comes A Time make this night stand out.  However the SBD suffers from recurring piano mic distortion that flares up especially on disc 3, making listening uncomfortable for some fans.  Musically it’s a solid Fall ’71 show; technically it’s only fair.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers love the musical content (≈4‑star show quality) but repeatedly note piano distortion that drags recording quality down to about fair; combined weighted score yields ~3.6.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Intermittent static/distortion in Keith's piano mic, especially on disc 3; distortion comes and goes but can be annoying. Apart from the keyboard issues the mix is otherwise clean with no audience patches.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Setlist mirrors the following night with added Bertha and Black Peter; the second half builds a powerful arc through The Other One (with cryptical sections) into a heartfelt Comes A Time.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Briefly noted as “knowing” the appropriate moment for Comes A Time, no specific performance comments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Frequent reports of piano channel distortion/static; Keith’s amp/signal appears to have overloaded the board, causing audible problems throughout much of the show.
